title: Changing Case
intro: Case operators transform text without retyping it: `gU` uppercases, `gu` lowercases, `g~` toggles. Combine them with a motion or text object: `gUiw` uppercases a word, `guu` lowercases the whole line, `gUU` uppercases it.

exercises.instruction: The constant name should be shouty. The cursor is on `max`. Uppercase the whole word with `gUiw`.
hints: `gU` is the uppercase operator; `iw` is the "inner word" text object.

exercises.instruction: This shouting should stop. Lowercase the entire line with `guu`.
hints: `guu` (like `dd` or `yy`, the doubled operator) applies to the whole line.

exercises.instruction: Toggle the case of the whole line with `g~~`.
hints: `g~~` flips every letter's case on the current line.

exercises.instruction: Uppercase from the cursor to the end of the line with `gU$`.
hints: `gU` takes any motion — `$` means "to end of line".
